reasoning: I will provide a concise summary of the article's main points and quotes without exposing internal chain-of-thought.
output: Backstage at the Baftas in London, Emma Stone’s Mia Dolan line from La La Land sets the tone as winners and attendees share moments and remarks. Tom Holland jokes about the downsides of playing Spider-Man, noting his legs are tired from spider poses after a whirlwind rise. Viola Davis defends Meryl Streep against critics, praising her decades of excellence, while Casey Affleck expresses admiration for Streep’s Globes speech and its impact. Dev Patel reflects on winning for Lion and his Skins debut, recounting how he started in the industry. Justin Hurwitz reveals a discarded opening song that didn’t make the film, and confirms Another Day of Sun was reinserted after a long cut. Mel Brooks receives the Bafta Fellow­ship and comments on Donald Trump, then promotes his West End-bound Young Frankenstein. The piece closes with social media details for BBC Entertainment News.
